checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AR growth ~55% (2015) and ~44% (2016) vs revenue growth ~24-32% and ~21%, respectively, based on above figures
evidence.location: derived from AccountsReceivableNetCurrent and SalesRevenueGoodsNet series

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=305564000 (2014-12-31) growing to PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=826868000 (2018-12-31), a ~2.7x increase
Revenues=5193185000 (FY2018) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=3084370000 (FY2014), a ~1.68x increase over the same period
Goodwill jumped from Goodwill=123256000 (2014-12-31) to Goodwill=595492000 (2015-03-31) due to acquisitions, then remained roughly flat through Goodwill=548762000 (2019-06-30)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=1158548000 (2017-12-31) vs InventoryNet=917491000 (2016-12-31), a +26.3% increase, while Revenues=4989244000 (FY2017) vs 4833338000 (FY2016) grew only +3.2%
GrossProfit=1512206000 / Revenue ~3084370000 = ~49.0% (FY2014) declining to GrossProfit=2340471000 / Revenues=5193185000 = ~45.1% (FY2018)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2018 income statement, derived margin trend

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=409679000 (2016-12-31) growing to AccountsPayableCurrent=561108000 (2017-12-31) and AccountsPayableCurrent=560884000 (2018-12-31), roughly tracking CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold=2584724000 (FY2016), 2737830000 (FY2017), 2852714000 (FY2018)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2013-02-25 followed immediately by a 10-K/A filed 2013-02-26

Repeated SEC CORRESP and UPLOAD filings clustered in 2011-2012 and again in 2017-2018 (e.g., UPLOAD 2017-05-01, CORRESP 2017-05-04, CORRESP 2017-05-30, UPLOAD 2018-05-23, CORRESP 2018-05-30, CORRESP 2018-06-15) consistent with SEC comment-letter review activity around periods of known revenue-recognition scrutiny

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual XBRL data series available continuously from 2009-06-30 through 2019-06-30 across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, InventoryNet, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, cash flow statement items, and filing chronology from 2005 through 2019-10-22
evidence.location: aggregate data coverage across financial_series_point_in_time and filing_chronology

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Premature/aggressive revenue recognition via 'pull-forward' of sales to wholesale/retail partners ahead of quarter-end to meet growth targets, recorded as revenue with corresponding receivables that outpaced revenue growth, later unwinding as reported revenue growth collapsed from double-digit rates to low single digits

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=622685000 (2016-12-31) vs 433638000 (2015-12-31), +43.6% while SalesRevenueGoodsNet=4646449000 (FY2016) grew ~21.4% over prior year 3825691000 (FY2015)

Revenues=4989244000 (FY2017) grew only ~3.2% over 4833338000 (FY2016), a sharp deceleration coinciding with AR normalization: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=609670000 (2017-12-31) roughly flat vs 622685000 (2016-12-31)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Delayed inventory write-downs/markdowns relative to buildup of excess/aged inventory, evidenced by inventory growing far faster than revenue in 2017 and a multi-year steady gross margin decline, suggesting inventory reserves and cost of goods sold did not fully reflect declining net realizable value in the periods reported

rationale_evidence.quote: Gross margin declined from GrossProfit=1512206000/Revenue~3084370000 (~49.0%, FY2014) to GrossProfit=2340471000/Revenues=5193185000 (~45.1%, FY2018)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large and growing divergence between reported net income/loss and operating cash flow suggests significant non-cash charges (restructuring, impairment, inventory reserves) and/or working-capital timing shifts obscuring the true trend of underlying profitability, warranting scrutiny of the size/timing/classification of restructuring and impairment charges recorded in 2017-2018

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew far faster than revenue in FY2015 (+55% vs revenue growth) and FY2016 (+44% vs +21% revenue growth), consistent with reported channel-stuffing/pull-forward concerns
Inventory grew +26.3% in FY2017 while revenue grew only +3.2%, alongside a persistent multi-year gross margin decline from ~49% (2014) to ~45% (2018)

Large and widening divergence between net income (turning negative in FY2017-FY2018) and strongly positive operating cash flow (FY2017: $237.46M CFO vs -$48.26M NI; FY2018: $628.23M CFO vs -$46.30M NI)

Filing chronology shows a same-week 10-K/10-K/A correction (Feb 2013) and clusters of SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence in 2011-2012 and 2017-2018 coinciding with periods of revenue-recognition scrutiny
